summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) People Program Manager who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing programs grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve the employee and candidate experience—through human-centered, data-driven approaches.

bullets.text: Own end-to-end planning and {coord|logistics} for 20+ programs and live {coord|events}, managing vendors, scheduling, and run-of-show
Build scalable processes, templates, and {coord|systems} that improve consistency and efficiency across a busy programming calendar
Facilitate {coord|events and webinars} for 600+ participants, creating memorable experiences that achieved a 78% program enrollment rate
Partner {cross|cross-functionally} with Marketing, Tech, and Sales to define goals, measure success, and shape engaging programming
Owned {coord|recruiting events} end-to-end and moderated C-suite panels at top 3 universities, reaching 230+ students and 80+ applicants
Engaged {ex|candidates} via personalized communication and customer service, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
Developed proficiency across HR functions, ranging from recruitment and compensation to performance management
Drafted job descriptions, posted openings, and researched global compensation to support employer-brand activations
Strengthened {prog|culture} via an HR newsletter and award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ competitors
Piloted an Inclusive Leadership workshop for 12 senior leaders, later scaled to 23 global locations
Optimized onboarding processes across 3 global locations by conducting process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities
Delivered strategic HR solutions for business priorities across projects in employee relations, headcount, and succession planning
Analyzed 2,000+ employee experiences and gathered {data|feedback} to continuously improve the employee experience
Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming {data|feedback data} into actionable reports that informed engagement strategies
Provided consultations to improve strategic plans and internal training on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review
Improved {ex|employee experience} via 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects focusing on survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D
Facilitate CEO/HR discovery and stakeholder interviews to diagnose drivers and design forward-looking culture and engagement plans
Empower HR to present executive-ready insights on $362K in leave costs, leading to policy redesigns and first aid training

changeLog: 2026-05-30: Tailored for Ramp — Senior People Events & Culture Manager (NYC; own internal + recruiting events end-to-end, vendor/budget/logistics, culture, measure & improve). Header → New York, NY (Open to Relocation). Title noun → People Program Manager. Lead InGenius (Set B events emphasis: end-to-end event planning + logistics + vendors + run-of-show, scalable templates/systems, facilitate events for 600+, cross-functional goal-setting) then Kronos (owned recruiting events end-to-end, employer-brand activations). Vestas → culture/well-being FIRST. ODN Pair B (forward-looking culture/engagement). Skills → added Vendor & Budget Management, Run-of-Show & Logistics, Internal Communications, Ashby.
